SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being

MENU

SDG 3.2 Number Graduating in Health Professions

SDG 3.2.1 Proportion of Graduates in Health Professions

SDG 3.3 Collaborations and Health Services

SDG 3.3.1  Current Collaborations with Health Institutions

Istanbul Okan University actively collaborates with local, national, and global health institutions to improve health and well-being outcomes. Through partnerships with hospitals, healthcare organizations, and research institutes, the University engages in initiatives that promote public health, support community well-being, and contribute to the development of healthcare services.

Collaborations in the Health Sector

SDG 3.3.2 Health Outreach Programmes

Istanbul Okan University actively implements healthcare outreach programmes and projects to promote health and well-being within the local community. These programmes include student volunteer initiatives focusing on topics such as hygiene, nutrition, family planning, sports, exercise, and healthy ageing. Through interactive workshops, seminars, and health campaigns, the University’s initiatives aim to raise awareness, educate the community, and support healthier lifestyles.

Gallery

The on-campus infirmary, supported by the Faculty of Medicine, operates five days a week and is staffed by two nurses and two general practitioners. To support both physical and mental health and to raise awareness on health-related issues, informational health brochures have been prepared for students, academic and administrative staff, as well as cleaning and security personnel. A nurse and an ambulance are available on campus 24/7, and an occupational physician provides healthcare services during weekday working hours.  The University infirmary offers essential healthcare services such as examinations and outpatient treatments for students and staff, and it also monitors patients who are referred to external healthcare centres. In addition, the University operates four fully equipped hospitals and one dental hospital.

SDG 3.3.3 Shared Sports Facilities

Istanbul Okan University shares its sports facilities with the local community, including local schools and the general public. The University’s sports complex is accessible for community use and supports activities such as sporting events, training sessions, and recreational programmes. This approach aims to promote a healthy and active lifestyle for both students and local residents.

Gallery

SDG 3.3.4  Sexual and Reproductive Health Care Services for Students

Istanbul Okan University provides students with access to sexual and reproductive health services. These services include information and educational support. The University offers counselling, workshops, and seminars on topics such as sexual health, family planning, and safe practices, ensuring that students have the necessary resources and guidance to make informed decisions about their health and well-being.

Gender Equality Seminars

SDG 3.3.5  Mental Health Support for Students

Istanbul Okan University provides students with comprehensive mental health support. The University offers counselling services, psychological assistance, and mental health workshops to promote students’ emotional well-being. Through these resources, students can seek help, receive guidance, and develop strategies such as stress management to maintain a healthy mental state throughout their academic journey.

OPDEM

SDG 3.3.6  Smoke-free Policy

Istanbul Okan University implements a “smoke-free campus” policy that prohibits smoking in all indoor areas. This policy aims to create a healthier environment for students, staff, and visitors, thereby supporting overall well-being on campus.

Smoke Free Policy

SDG 3.3.7  Mental Health Support for Staff

Istanbul Okan University provides mental health support for its staff members. The University offers counselling services, mental health resources, and workshops to promote the psychological well-being of its personnel. These services ensure that staff receive the support they need to maintain a healthy work–life balance.
